_comment: Material-aware taxonomy for NYLON WATCH. Each material block: terms that RAISE fit when present in title/tag/type, plus 'avoid' terms that lower it. 'weight' scales matches. 'enabled' is the default; the app overrides per-material via its own toggle state. The visual gloss stage is separate from all of this and configured under 'visual'.

visual._note: If a product matches NO material keyword but its image gloss >= gloss_floor_for_unkeyworded, it is kept and flagged GLOSSY — this is what catches shiny garments whose listings never say so. max_images_per_run caps work so the Action stays fast/free; newest + highest-keyword items are imaged first.

tone_ranking._note: Encodes the gloss PRIORITY order from image tone, not fiber. Each kept item gets a tone (colored/metallic/wetlook_black/black_satin/neutral) and a 'desire' = gloss + the matching bonus below, which drives the default 'Best' sort. Signals: litsat (lit-band saturation) => colored; subjval (subject brightness) => metallic(bright) vs black(dark); specden (highlight tightness) => wet-look(sharp) vs satin(soft). Colored jewel-tone and metallic/silver are rewarded; black true wet-look is neutral; black SATIN (dark + colorless + soft sheen) is penalized because it reads flat/cheap. Tune the thresholds/bonuses here; items with no image data stay neutral. This only reorders (and, via the app's 'Priority' filter, can hide) — it never changes what the poller keeps.

nylon._note: keyword_keep:false — 'nylon' and technical/brand labels are NOT a reliable shine signal (most technical nylon is matte). These terms only TAG an item for display/filtering; a nylon piece is kept ONLY if its photo actually scores glossy. This is the fix for the matte-nylon flood.

rainwear._note: keyword_keep:false — plain rain/waterproof labels are often matte, so rainwear terms only TAG for display; a rain piece is kept only if the photo scores glossy (or it also hits a genuine shine word like 'coated'/'lacquer', which live in wetlook). High-gloss PU rainwear like Rains still surfaces via its shiny image.

disqualifiers._note: SILHOUETTE / HARDWARE VETO. gloss.py judges sheen, not shape, so latex/PVC/fetish shops throw contoured, hardware-heavy, cut-to-expose false positives that the gloss score alone can't reject. A product whose title/description matches ANY term here is dropped by the poller BEFORE it renders or gets an image scored, no matter how glossy — the aesthetic wants boxy/baggy real-clothes cuts (button-ups, tees, track jackets, trousers, shorts), not fetish garments. This list is a coarse TEXT proxy for shape and is meant to be edited: remove a term if it's cutting real clothes you want, add one if junk gets through. Terms were chosen to avoid false hits on desirable pieces (e.g. 'cage' is omitted because it's inside 'cagoule'; bare 'crotch' is omitted because technical trousers list a gusseted crotch; 'chest zip' is omitted because tracked running shops use chest-zip pockets).
